paper.title: Sapaki: Galactic O3If* star possibly born in isolation

review.summary: Single-object spectroscopic study of O3If* star Sapaki (WR67a). Not a catalog paper. Contains 2 LaTeX tables: (1) literature astrometric/photometric data for a single target, (2) radial velocity line-center measurements. No machine-readable tables, no data-availability resources, no external data files. Paper uses data from existing public catalogs (Gaia DR3, 2MASS, WISE, TIC, GLIMPSE) - these are generic acknowledgements, not unique resources.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Literature astrometric and photometric data compiled for the single target star Sapaki from public catalogs (Gaia DR3, TIC, 2MASS, WISE, GLIMPSE). Used as input for extinction and distance calculations.

source_refs.caption: Literature data for Sapaki. Coordinates, parallaxes, proper motions, and G-band photometry were retrieved from Gaia Data Release 3 (Gaia Collaboration \citeyear{Gaia2022}). The $B-$ and $V$-band photometry values were taken from the TESS Input Catalog V8.2 (Paegert et al. \citeyear{Petal2021}). $J$-, $H$- and $K_{s}$-band photometry was taken from the 2MASS Point Source Catalog (Skrutskie et al. \citeyear{Setal2006}). W1-band and W2-band photometry values were taken from the WISE All-Sky Data Release (Cutri et al. \citeyear{Cetal2012}), while the [8.0] $\mu$m photometry was taken from the GLIMPSE Source Catalog (Benjamin et al. \citeyear{Betal2003})
source_refs.label: tab:Sapaki_astrophot

columns.name: Sapaki
columns.meaning: Measured value for the target star Sapaki

internal_tables.evidence: Two-column table listing parameter names (ID column) and corresponding values (Sapaki column). Parameters include: equatorial coordinates (alpha, delta), Galactic coordinates (l, b), Gaia DR3 parallax and proper motions, and multi-band photometry (B, V, G, J, H, Ks, W1, W2, [8.0]).
internal_tables.comments: Single-row table for one target. Data compiled from Gaia DR3, TIC v8.2, 2MASS PSC, WISE All-Sky, and GLIMPSE Source Catalog.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Radial velocity measurements from 10 spectral lines observed with MagE. Used to compute the heliocentric radial velocity of Sapaki (Vr = -34.2 +/- 15.6 km/s).

source_refs.caption: Line-center measurements of several absorption/emission present in the atmosphere of Sapaki. $\lambda_{0}$ is the air wavelength, $\lambda_{\text{obs}}$ is the measured wavelength with \texttt{splot} routine in IRAF, $\Delta\lambda = \lambda_{\text{obs}} - \lambda_{0}$, and $V_{r} = c \Delta\lambda/\lambda_{\lambda_{0}}$, where $c = 2.99 \times 10^{5}$ km/s.
source_refs.label: tab:Sapaki_vr

columns.name: lambda_0
columns.meaning: Rest (air) wavelength of the spectral line

columns.name: lambda_obs
columns.meaning: Observed wavelength measured with IRAF splot routine

columns.name: V_r
columns.meaning: Radial velocity derived from the wavelength shift

internal_tables.evidence: Table contains 10 spectral line measurements (N IV, Si IV, Hdelta, He II, Hgamma, He II, N V, N V, Hbeta, He II) with their rest wavelengths, observed wavelengths, wavelength shifts, and derived radial velocities. Typical measurement uncertainty is +/- 0.1 Angstrom in lambda_obs, translating to +/- 5 km/s systematics per line.
internal_tables.comments: Lines measured using both Gaussian and Voigt profile fitting via IRAF splot. Each line measured at least 10 times.

external_resources.description: CarPy (Carnegie Python) MagE pipeline used for raw spectral data reduction

source_refs.context: The raw frames were reduced by using the MagE pipeline, which is part of the Carnegie Python distribution (CarPy\footnote{\href{https://code.obs.carnegiescience.edu/mage-pipeline}{https://code.obs.carnegiescience.edu/mage-pipeline}}).

external_resources.evidence: Mentioned in Section 2 (Observations and data reduction) as the data reduction pipeline for MagE spectra.
external_resources.comments: Software/data-reduction resource, not a data product.

external_resources.description: IRAF (Image Reduction and Analysis Facility) data reduction software

source_refs.context: In addition, the data were later retouched with standard IRAF\footnote{\href{http://iraf.noao.edu/}{http://iraf.net/}} routines.

external_resources.evidence: Mentioned in Section 2 as software used for spectral data processing. Also acknowledged at end of paper.
external_resources.comments: Software resource. IRAF is distributed by NOAO.

external_resources.evidence: Alternate URL for IRAF mentioned in footnote.
external_resources.comments: Software resource. Same resource as resource-iraf-noao but different URL.
